_meta.title: Opis SA taxonomy v1 (assembled from published ontologies)
_meta.purpose: Phase-1 deliverable: the literature-grounded TARGET that business domain terms map onto. Axes: term (domain-facing, slot_types), logic (join/branch), template-movement (EIP), template-computation (Dataflow+DDD), kind (resilience). See ontology/corpus/*.md for sources and induced_ontology_v1.json for the full 43-pattern logic-axis induction.
_meta.domain_to_SA_mapping: A kata's business behaviour resolves to a tuple: (1) a TERM (its data, via slot_types extends), (2) a TEMPLATE — either a MOVEMENT template (EIP: route/filter/aggregate/split/enrich/observe) or a COMPUTATION template (Dataflow ParDo/Combine/window + DDD Aggregate/Domain-Service for compute & state), (3) a LOGIC (how it joins inputs), (4) a KIND (stability role). Phase-1 goal is to exercise this mapping across many katas.

@graph.name: term
@graph.sourceOntology: Opis slot_types (domain-facing); OWL-S IOPE / FIBO / FRO for grounding if needed
@graph.note: the business-domain-facing axis: what data flows. Business terms map here via `extends`. Not re-derived in this run.

@graph.name: logic
@graph.sourceOntology: van der Aalst / Russell Workflow Control-Flow Patterns (43 patterns)
@graph.note: how a gate joins/branches its inputs. Full induction in induced_ontology_v1.json.

@graph.name: template_movement
@graph.sourceOntology: Hohpe & Woolf Enterprise Integration Patterns (65 patterns)
@graph.note: MOVEMENT templates: how a gate routes/filters/aggregates/splits/enriches/observes messages.

@graph.name: template_computation
@graph.sourceOntology: Akidau et al. Dataflow Model (PVLDB 2015) + Evans DDD tactical patterns
@graph.note: COMPUTATION templates: what NEW VALUE a gate computes / what STATE it owns. Added 2026-07-04 after silicon mapping found EIP has no home for compute/accumulate gates.

@graph.name: kind
@graph.sourceOntology: Nygard Release It! + Azure Cloud Design Patterns (resilience)
@graph.note: a gate's stability role: how it protects the flow.
FIRST = THRESHOLD(n=1); Discriminator = 1-of-m Partial Join
the TEMPLATE form of AND/THRESHOLD logic: completeness condition + correlation. Unifies logic and template axes.
Opis discourages silent drops (ADR-011 rejected sink gate); guard emits reject rather than discard
runtime-mutable routing; no Opis template (routing static in a flow). GAP.
ordering guarantee; likely CA/body layer. GAP.
buffered/retained input; same buffering gap as WCP24 Persistent Trigger. GAP.
resolves silicon friction C: a gate that computes a NEW value from inputs, stateless.
resolves silicon friction D: TWO patterns — Combine (what it folds) + Aggregate (which entity owns the state). A stateful gate declares both.
reduce scoped to a time window; SECOND independent grounding of Opis windows (input_timeout_ms) on the compute side — resilience Timeout grounds them on the protection side.
computation whose output is not owned by any single input term; grounds cross-term compute gates that ParDo alone under-describes.
computed, immutable, identity-less output terms: estimate, price, routing_decision. Distinct from entity terms.
identity-bearing terms (order, customer, driver); the natural aggregate roots for stateful gates.
trips_on = failure threshold; recovery_ms = timeout to half-open. Source definition.
brokering/validation guard in front of a protected gate. Source definition. auth_token realizes it.
per-compartment resource isolation; candidate refinement of regulator. Only kind-axis concept without a clean Opis primitive.

@graph.groundedIn: Fail Fast (Nygard) for the guard/validation case
Timeout (Nygard); NOT in workflow-patterns (untimed) or EIP
@graph.note: IMPORTANT nuance: windows are Opis-original to WORKFLOW modeling (that literature is untimed) but STANDARD in resilience engineering (Timeout pattern). State precisely, do not overclaim novelty.

@graph.groundedIn: Circuit Breaker recovery timeout; Governor (rate limit); rider-cooldown
@graph.note: timed refractory after firing. Grounded in resilience, absent from workflow/EIP.
outcomes carry probabilities for twin sampling. No counterpart in ANY of the three source ontologies. Genuine Opis contribution (measurement/twin layer).

@graph.groundedIn: Timeout (partial — Timeout bounds waits but is not a distributional budget)
@graph.note: distributional timing contract on a gate; twin-measured. Closest is Timeout but that is a hard bound, not a p50/p95 distribution.
